text.title: Kill Criteria Template Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every Kill Criteria Template application must enforce. These are HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override them.

rule.statement: Kill thresholds MUST be written BEFORE launch event; post-launch thresholds are rejected.
rule.rationale: Pre-registration removes negotiate-with-reality bias when emotional sunk cost grows.

rule.statement: Each kill card MUST name exactly ONE primary metric (MRR, MAU, signups, or joy-score); multi-metric AND/OR clauses are rejected.
rule.rationale: Multi-metric kills are dodge-able — every metric becomes an excuse to wait one more cycle.

rule.statement: Kill card MUST carry a fixed review date (T+30 / T+60 / T+90 from launch); ad-hoc reviews are rejected.
rule.rationale: Calendar-locked review removes 'let me give it one more week' loops.

rule.statement: Kill card MUST carry a single named owner (human); team/collective owners are rejected.

rationale.source: Operational hygiene (Allspaw, On Being a Senior Engineer)

rule.rationale: Un-owned kill criteria rot — nobody runs the review.

rule.statement: Every threshold MUST cite the baseline number it was derived from; free-floating thresholds are rejected.
rule.rationale: Threshold without baseline = wishful thinking; the founder picks a number that feels OK.
